title: doxantha unguis-cati var. exoleta
intro: Doxantha unguis-cati var. exoleta, also known as cat's claw vine, is a climbing plant native to Central and South America, characterized by its thorny stems and claw-like roots. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are derived from its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Doxantha unguis-cati var. exoleta include reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Traditionally, this plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in some herbal supplements, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent require further research.
